steps.title: Identify the form
steps.content: $S(t)=8(1.6)^{t}$ is an exponential function of the form $a\cdot b^{t}$, where $a$ is the initial value and $b$ is the growth factor. Here $a=8$ and $b=1.6$.

steps.title: Evaluate at $t=0$
steps.content: Since $t$ counts years since the newsletter launched, plug in $t=0$: $S(0)=8(1.6)^{0}=8\cdot 1=8$. So $8$ is the number of subscribers when the newsletter launched.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• A) The estimated number of subscribers when the newsletter launched. This equals $S(0)=8$, which is exactly the role the constant $8$ plays.
  • B) The increase in the estimated number of subscribers each year. The yearly multiplier is $1.6$, not a constant additive increase, so $8$ does not describe an annual increase.
  • Choice C — The number of years the newsletter has been active The number of years the newsletter has been active. That is the variable $t$, not the constant $8$.
  • D) The percent increase in the estimated number of subscribers each year. That comes from $b-1=0.6$, i.e., $60\%$, not from the $8$.
That confirms A is correct.
